mnemonics: P.E.T.S. - Polynomials: Exponents, Terms, Sorted (by degree)
C.A.T.S. - Coefficients Are The Scalars (multiplying each term)
F.O.I.L. - First, Outer, Inner, Last (for multiplying binomials)
keyPoints: A polynomial is composed of terms, each with a coefficient and a non-negative integer exponent.
The degree of a polynomial is the highest exponent of any term.
Polynomials can be represented in code using arrays or linked lists where the index or node data represents the exponent.
Operations include addition, subtraction, multiplication, and evaluation (using Horner's Rule for efficiency).
commonMistakes: Forgetting to sort polynomial terms by their exponent (ascending or descending) before performing operations.
Incorrectly handling the addition or multiplication of coefficients for like terms.
Using an inefficient evaluation method (naive method) instead of Horner's Rule, which reduces the number of multiplications.
